# ++CHRCH Access

*Section: THE BOOK OF APPENDIX: Resources To Help You Rise*

---

##    🔑 1. NINE RECOMMENDED BOOKS FOR BUILDING CREATIVE ENERGY 

_I've read all these books multiple times. Good books should be studied if you really want to get the gold and the silver out of them. I've recommended these to others who have also had their lives changed by them. So, you can invest in all 9 of them knowing they are "risk-free" energy builders._

#   


# **1\. The Alchemist**

**Paulo Coelho  **

My favorite book of all time. There are so many secrets unlocks in this book. I read it every year.

  


# **2\. The War Of Art**

**Steven Pressfield**

This is the book I've read that most closely resembles The Mystery School philosophy. How to conquer your creative demons.

  


# **3\. The Process Of Creating Life**

**Christopher Alexander**

The Godfather of Architechture explains how PROCESS affects the outcome. And how your PROCESS is the difference between creating something that is _radiating full of life_... And creating something that is _dead on arrival_. This book is meditative. And it may even make you cry.

  


# **4\. Flow**

**Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi**

The absolute bible on how to achieve the state of mind that every artist wishes they could stay in forever.

  


# **5\. Prince and the Purple Rain Era Studio Sessions: 1983 and 1984**

**Duane Tudhal**

A full documentation of the recording sessions for 1999 and Purple. Day by Day. A guide on how to become the most prolific artist that you can possibly be.

  


# **6\. Po: Beyond Yes And No**

**Edward de Bono**

The best description of what happens inside a healthy creative person's mind.

  


# **7\. On Writing**

**Stephen King**

Don't let the title fool you. This is a book for ALL creatives.

  


# **8\. Preface To The Picture Of Dorian Gray**

**Oscar Wilde**

You don't need to read the whole book. Just read the preface.

  


# **9\. Self-Reliance**

**Ralph Waldo Emerson**

If this short essay doesn't get you back on track every time, maybe you weren't made for this.

  


# **VERY HONORABLE MENTIONS**

 _I've read all of these books multiple times also, but they're not automatic recommendations only because it depends on where you are on your journey._

  


**Atomic Habits**

James Clear

The bible of how to form new positive habits.

  


**Why We Sleep**

Matthew Walker

The modern bible on sleep. And how it affects your creativity.

  


**Zero To One**

Blake Masters and Peter Thiel

A scientific look at how to create ideas that have never existed before.

  


**The Alter Ego Effect: The Power Of Secret Identities To Transform Your Life**

Todd Herman

How entertainers, artists, and athletes use alter-egos to help them do the things they are too afraid to do normally.

  


**The Master Game: Beyond the Drug Experience  **

Robert S. de Ropp

My friend Bruce introduced me to this book. It explains The 4th State Of Consciousness. The secret to mastering the shadow self.

  


**Autobiography Of A Yogi**

Paramahansa Yogananda

I re-read Chapter 12 over and over again. Unfortunately for it to have it's full effect on you, you have to read everything leading up to it. But if you invest that time... When Chapter 12 arrives it is just pure truth. (This was also known as Steve Jobs's favorite book.)

  


**Purple Cow**

Seth Godin

A book to remind you why being original is more likely to make you successful in a world full of "copies of a copy".

  


**Siddhartha**

Hermann Hesse

A novel about following your own path while everyone else is following gurus.

  


**The Andy Warhol Diaries**

Pat Hackett

One of the most prolific artists who ever lived also made time to document every day. 

  


**Rebel without a Crew: Or How a 23-Year-Old Filmmaker With $7,000 Became a Hollywood Player**

Robert Rodriguez

Shows you to what extent someone can go to to realize their vision when they seem to have no resources.

  


**The Beautiful Fall**

Alicia Drake

Watch the complete opposite creative approach of 2 fashion geniuses.

  


  


# **STUDENT RECOMMENDED:**

  


**The Rise of Superman: Decoding the Science of Ultimate Human Performance  **

Steven Kotler

Recommended by Dave: _"I’ve actually been interested in flow for almost a decade now after someone explained it to me. I’ve always “known” what flow was, and it’s always been my ultimate goal to get better and better at just flipping that switch and being in it.  My absolute favorite book on flow is “The Rise of Superman” by Steven Kotler because it doesn’t read like a textbook, it reads like an exciting adventure that makes you wonder just how amazing the human body and mind is and if we’re capable of so much more than we allow of ourselves."_

  


**Start with Why**

Simon Sinek

Recommended by Freddy Kuno: _"It's popular among Marketing and Entrepreneur folks, but the message is essentially the same as The Drill Down Nethod: you (and people) need to know fucking WHY you do what you do. WHY should anyone listen to you and not to someone else? Follow you and not anyone else? It will also help any creative working for big brands to think about this."_

  


**_Amusing Ourselves to Death: Public Discourse in the Age of Show Business_**

Neil Postman

Recommended by Wesley: _"It was written in 1985, but I think it’s extremely relevant to the modern media landscape. In the book he argues that “a brave new world” by Aldous Huxley (and it's SOMA drug) is a much better prediction of the modern world compared to “1984”. I find it interesting and somewhat ironic that 1984 is taught in schools as almost a way of instilling fear of a totalitarian government and thoughts censorship, when our current reality is arguably worse."_

  


**Consider This: Moments in My Writing Life After Which Everything Was Different**

Chuck Palahniuk

Recommended by Tomson: _"__Yup. The fight club guy. But wow. It is really The Mystery School! His anecdotes... His obsession with testing his writings with his audience... One of my favorite quotes: "__Witness the movies that premiered to damning reviews. The Night of the Living Dead. Harold and Maude. Blade Runner. They found a place in public memory, and time has made them classics. So do not write to be liked. Write to be remembered."_

  


**Scary Close**

Donald Miller. 

Recommended by Haley: _"This book will challenge the way you love and the way you receive it. It’s a book about giving up the act and picking up true intimacy in this life."_

  


**Big Magic**

Elizabeth Gilbert 

Recommended by Ines: _"She really shows you how the low moments are integral part of the process. And how if you don't act on inspiration the moment when it hits you, it will leave. Full of stories to back her points up too..."_

  


**Daily Rituals: How Artists Work**

**Daily Rituals: Women At Work**

Mason Currey 

Recommended by Ines: _"Mason Currey wrote a book called Daily rituals: How artists work. It talks about known artists in all creative fields and what are/were their routines / disciplines / rituals. While I was deciding if it's anything worth buying, I found out that he came across an issue - he got a lot of feedback on the fact that there's only a few women mentioned in the book. So he put together another one called Daily rituals: Women at work. So instead, I bought that one. And it is a gem. So many inspiring stories of creatives I hadn't even heard of, but that really should be celebrated. It's a treasure trove of inspiring quotes on creatives' lives."  _

  


**Letters to a Young Poet**

Rainer Maria Rilke

Recommended by Jillian: _"This book always puts everything back into perspective for me. It emphasizes the importance of being alone to look within yourself, and is such an insightful collection of letters and advice."_

  


**The Untethered Soul**

**The Surrender Experiment**

Michael Singer

Recommended by David: _"These books go very well with the book of mental health. Being able to not get lost in your emotions is a great tool for an artist imho, and Singer's books have helped me so much in dealing with those "dark nights of the soul"_

  


**Zen and the Art of Motorcycle Maintenance**

Robert M. Pirsig

Recommended by Rowen: _"I've avoided this book for a while but when it popped up in a free bin at a random coffee shop, all torn to shit and written over, I had to pick it up. I always thought this book was just some pop psych pulp phenom, full of bullshit platitudes and half baked ideas. Plot twist, it's not that at all."_

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/34246945/comments/22668416)

WHY ARE YOU CREATIVE, by Herman Vaske Vaske is a well known figure in the ad industry (well, at least WAS, back in the late 90s and 2000s) He did interviews with top creatives from the ad industry, published in LÜRZER'S ARCHIVE. He expanded to artists, musicians, etc. Made even a film interviewing those peepz and now has this book with 100 interviews. Can only recommend it. There's an older, other version of this book, too: STANDING ON THE SHOULDER OF GIANTS. The peeps interviewed drop some serious wisdom.

Art & Fear: Observations on the Perils (and Rewards) of Artmaking by David Bayles & Chris Orland Explores the way art gets made, the reasons it often doesn't get made, and the nature of the difficulties that cause so many artists to give up along the way. Very inspiring! Very mystery school

![Francesca Mey](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/a55da37cad510d00782dcdc3e6a5619d?d=mm&s=70)

Francesca Mey

Awaiting Review 

3 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/34246945/comments/21298090)

Great list! Some other suggestions: \- "Tao Te Ching" Lao Tzu \- Rick Rubin's "The Creative Act" \- "The Perfectionist's Guide to Losing Control" by Katherine Morgan Schafler \- "12 Notes: On Life and Creativity" by Quincy Jones

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/34246945/comments/21019675)

Currently reading "The mountain is you: turning self-sabotage into self-mastery" by Brianna Wiest and she is basically preaching everything Father bronques says...it's such a good supplemental read to these modules. A few pages before I get to work paired with celery juice in the morning is my new routine. My toxic trait is self-sabatoge so this book is really helping me break free to make my videos the way I want to.

Ok... Your email this morning about me losing faith was absolutely correct, and at the same time my faith is fundamental so when I lose it, I struggle a lot. The email made me immediately jump on, and I already purchased three of those books.
